figures.caption: Fig. 3: The naive visual attention amplification method exacerbates visual inertia. A visual activeness comparison between (a) the baseline model and (b) the naive attention amplification method PAI [32] shows that the amplification strategy reduces visual attention activeness, thereby triggering cognitive hallucinations.
Fig. 10: An illustration of IVE mitigating cognitive hallucinations on Reefknot [63].

Fig. 1: Overview of the cognitive hallucinations and their mitigation via the proposed IVE. Upper Left: Comparison between perceptual and cognitive hallucinations in MLLMs. Upper Right: Comparison with hallucination mitigation methods across multiple benchmarks. Bottom: Compared to PAI [32] which amplifies visual attention designed primarily for mitigating perceptual hallucinations, our IVE effectively reduces both perceptual and cognitive hallucinations by exciting inertial visual attention.
Fig. 12: An illustration of IVE mitigating cognitive hallucinations on Reefknot [63].
Fig. 11: An illustration of IVE mitigating cognitive hallucinations on Reefknot [63].

Fig. 6: Visualization of visual activeness of each token, comparing hallucinated and non-hallucinated responses in cognitive hallucination tasks. Rows indicate layer indices in LLaVA-1.5 [30], and columns correspond to decoded tokens at each step.
Fig. 14: An illustration of IVE mitigating cognitive hallucinations on Reefknot [63].
Fig. 13: An illustration of IVE mitigating cognitive hallucinations on Reefknot [63].
Table 5: Comparison of inference latency and computational cost across various decoding strategies on the Reefknot benchmark [63].

Fig. 4: Overview of our proposed IVE framework. Left: The autoregressive generation process of MLLMs integrated with IVE, which operates on token attention during next-token prediction. Top Right: Trend-guided Token Selection partitions visual tokens according to their temporal deviation from historical attention trends, distinguishing dynamically emergent tokens from inertia tokens that maintain stable concentration patterns, thereby enabling adaptive emphasis on newly relevant visual regions. Bottom Right: Inertia-aware Attention Penalty quantifies the persistence of inertia tokens across decoding steps and progressively attenuates their influence, reallocating the penalized attention toward emergent tokens to discourage prolonged over-concentration on historically dominant regions.
Fig. 8: Attention heatmap comparison between our IVE and the naive attention amplification method PAI [32].
Fig. 7: Radar chart comparison of hallucination mitigation methods across multiple benchmarks under InstructBLIP [8] and Qwen2.5-VL-Instruct [2].

Table 4: Perceptual Hallucination Mitigation on POPE benchmark [25]. Accuracy and F1-score (%) comparison under different evaluation settings (i.e., Random, Popular and Adversarial). ∆ denotes improvement over vanilla baseline.
